sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) Declares $0.62 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 13, 2026. This marks the 15th consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, offering an annualized yield of 2.9%. While the current payout ratio is high at 82.9%, analysts anticipate an improved payout ratio of 67.8% next year based on projected EPS of $3.66.

feed.title: Molecular Cytogenetics Market Intelligence Report (2020-2030) Featuring Key Players Danaher, MetaSystems, Agilent Technologies, Abbott Labs, Bio-Rad Labs and More - ResearchAndMarkets.com
feed.summary: The global Molecular Cytogenetics Market, valued at USD 2.24 Billion in 2024, is projected to grow to USD 3.62 Billion by 2030, exhibiting a CAGR of 8.33%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing prevalence of genetic conditions and oncological diseases, alongside the rising demand for personalized medicine. Key challenges include the high cost of instrumentation and the need for specialized expertise, while key trends involve the integration of AI and automation to enhance efficiency and precision in workflows.

feed.title: Today & Tomorrow - Commercial, powering the integration engine %
feed.summary: This article discusses the role of Phillips 66's Commercial sector in powering the company's integration engine. It highlights how strong assets, global scale, and talent contribute to Commercial's impact, emphasizing its competitive differentiators, integrated downstream portfolio, and the capabilities of its people. The discussion features Brian Mandell, EVP of Marketing and Commercial, and Mark Hughes, SVP of Commercial, who explain how their approach creates a unique and diversified value proposition for Phillips 66 in global markets.

feed.title: Cooper Companies Likely To Report Higher Q4 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ: COO) is expected to report higher Q4 earnings of $1.11 per share and $1.06 billion in revenue after the market closes on Thursday, December 4th. This is an increase from the previous year's $1.04 per share and $1.02 billion revenue. The company's board also approved a $1 billion increase in its share repurchase program on September 17th.

feed.title: Atmos Energy director Donohue sells $77k in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Atmos Energy Director Sean Donohue sold 450 shares for approximately $77,017 on December 3, 2025, reducing his direct ownership to 876.598 shares. This insider transaction follows the company's strong fourth-quarter financial results, including an EPS of $1.07 and revenue of $754.64 million, which led Mizuho to raise its price target to $180.

feed.title: Walmart Inc. (WMT): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article presents a bull case for Walmart Inc. (WMT), highlighting its transformation into a digitally enabled, margin-expanding platform. The analysis emphasizes Walmart's omnichannel strategy, including leveraging its stores as fulfillment centers and developing higher-margin profit streams like advertising and Walmart+ memberships. The article suggests that Walmart's current valuation premium is justified by its strategic initiatives, despite the stock's sensitivity to execution risks.

feed.title: Big Tech Stocks: Earnings Season Winners and Losers
feed.summary: This article reviews the performance of several major tech stocks following their latest earnings reports, highlighting both winners and losers in a mixed market response to the AI boom. Alphabet emerged as a winner with an 8% surge due to strong AI execution, while Meta Platforms and Palantir saw declines despite strong results, primarily due to concerns over AI spending and high valuations, respectively. Nvidia also slipped after reporting, despite impressive earnings, which analysts attribute to broader market skepticism about AI valuations.

feed.title: NXP Semiconductors N.V. (VNX.DE): Riding a Significant Uptrend on Germany’s XETRA
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors N.V. (VNX.DE) is showing strong momentum on Germany's XETRA, with an 8.68% increase to €194.00, driven by robust trading volumes and favorable technical indicators. The company boasts solid financials, including a market cap of €49.49 billion and an ROE of 21.63%, despite challenges in the broader semiconductor sector. NXP's diverse product range and strategic position in automotive and IoT solutions contribute to its resilience and optimistic market outlook.

feed.title: Truist Securities Raises Price Target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) | HST Stock News
feed.summary: Truist Securities upgraded its price target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) to $21.00 from $19.00, while maintaining a 'Hold' rating. This adjustment reflects analyst Patrick Scholes' continued outlook on the stock. Host Hotels & Resorts is an owner of numerous upper-upscale and luxury hotel properties, primarily in the United States.

feed.title: Danaher (DHR) Valuation Check After Analyst Upgrade and 2026 Earnings Recovery Narrative
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's upgrade has put Danaher (DHR) on watchlists, fueled by optimism for a 2026 recovery through core revenue growth and earnings leverage. While currently appearing undervalued at $228.46 against a narrative fair value of $254.20, its P/E ratio of 46.1x suggests a tougher valuation story compared to its industry. The long-term narrative focuses on precision medicine and AI-assisted diagnostics driving EBITDA expansion, though risks like China policy shifts and biotech funding weakness could impact recovery.

feed.title: Keysight unveils new FieldFox handheld analyzer with 120 MHz streaming
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) has introduced its N99xxD-Series FieldFox Handheld Analyzer with 120 MHz gap-free IQ streaming, designed for complex radio frequency environments. The analyzer offers laboratory-grade precision with field-ready durability and AI capabilities for automatic classification of wireless standards. This launch follows strong fiscal fourth-quarter results for Keysight, with analysts raising price targets due to robust demand and growth in AI and data center markets.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Has Lowered Expectations for Chemours (NYSE:CC) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reduced its price target for Chemours (NYSE:CC) from $15.00 to $13.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which implies a minimal upside. This move reflects lowered expectations, consistent with other firms that have recently cut their price objectives for the specialty chemicals company. Chemours recently missed quarterly EPS estimates and reported a negative net margin, with its shares trading around $12.95.

feed.title: Medtronic’s Hugo surgical robot earns FDA clearance
feed.summary: Medtronic's Hugo surgical robot has received FDA clearance for urologic procedures in the U.S., positioning it as a significant challenger to Intuitive Surgical in the robotic surgery market. This authorization allows hospitals more choice in expanding their robotic programs, with plans to extend the robot's use to general surgery and gynecology in the future. The company anticipates this expansion will help revive growth in its medical/surgical portfolio and leverage its modular design and digital technologies as key differentiators.

feed.title: NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE), highlighting its strong financial and operational performance, consistent EPS growth outlook, and diversified portfolio including renewables and storage. The thesis emphasizes the company's ability to cater to increasing data-center and AI energy demand, with significant rerating potential despite identified risks.

feed.title: Why Palantir And Nvidia's Infrastructure Move Matters More Than Any Model
feed.summary: Palantir Technologies, in partnership with Nvidia and CenterPoint Energy, is shifting the focus of AI growth from model development to infrastructure through its "Chain Reaction" initiative. They argue that the bottleneck to AI innovation is now power and compute, not algorithms. This collaboration highlights a strategic pivot towards ensuring scalable energy and compute environments are available to sustain the rapidly expanding AI landscape, impacting future investment and defining the next phase of AI companies.

feed.title: Boston Scientific gets European nod for Farapoint catheter
feed.summary: Boston Scientific recently received CE mark for its Farapoint pulsed field ablation (PFA) catheter, allowing it to be marketed in Europe for treating right atrial flutter (AFL). This expands the indications for its Farapulse PFA platform, which was initially approved for AFib. The company aims to become a top player in the electrophysiology space, leveraging the Farapoint catheter's demonstrated efficacy and safety in preventing AFL recurrence.

feed.title: A $5B Tailwind: How Ukraine's Allies Are Poised to Boost Defense ETFs
feed.summary: The ongoing conflict in Ukraine is creating significant tailwinds for U.S. defense contractors and defense-focused ETFs. With direct U.S. aid paused, allied nations are committing billions towards purchasing U.S. weapons for Ukraine, ensuring a steady revenue stream for manufacturers. This shift emphasizes the strategic advantage of diversified defense ETFs for investors looking to capitalize on sustained global defense spending.

feed.title: EBay's New Sports Card Price Guide Could Change The Game for Collectors
feed.summary: EBay Inc. is launching a new pricing tool for trading cards, utilizing actual marketplace data to provide collectors with accurate valuations. This feature, accessible via the "My eBay" mobile app, allows users to scan cards and view two years of transaction history for both raw and graded items. The initiative aims to address historical challenges in pricing accuracy within the trading card market, a sector that significantly contributed to eBay's strong Q3 growth.

feed.title: RTX stock rises after Raytheon expands AWS collaboration for space operations
feed.summary: RTX Corp (NYSE:RTX) stock rose 1.8% after its Raytheon business announced an expanded strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS). This partnership aims to enhance national security capabilities by using scalable cloud-based solutions, AI, and machine learning for satellite data processing and mission control. The collaboration focuses on reducing mission costs, increasing flexibility, and accelerating capability delivery for government and commercial space sector customers.

feed.title: TJ Maxx (NYSE: TJX) debuts Maxxinista Express holiday bus tour across three cities
feed.summary: TJ Maxx (NYSE: TJX) has launched the "Maxxinista Express," a double-decker bus holiday tour in December 2025, taking superfans on full-day shopping trips to three TJ Maxx stores in Los Angeles, Dallas, and Miami. The experience includes contests, exclusive merchandise, photo opportunities, and celebrity hosts like Janel Parrish. This experiential marketing initiative aims to celebrate Maxxinista culture and enhance holiday shopping, complementing TJX's strong financial performance.

feed.title: Enterprise data integration shaping modern workflows
feed.summary: Enterprise data integration is becoming a critical component for businesses leveraging AI, as it helps connect disparate data sources and application stacks. Salesforce, with its Data 360 platform built on AWS, emphasizes an open ecosystem approach to allow customers flexibility while integrating their data for AI agents and various workflows. This strategy aims to simplify data management and activation across different clouds, fostering innovation in areas like voice-driven agents and collaborative tools.

feed.title: Workday Recognized as a Leader in 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Financial Planning Software for Fourth Year in a Row
feed.summary: Workday has been named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Planning Software for the fourth consecutive year, highlighting its AI-enabled platform for managing people, money, and agents. The company's Workday Adaptive Planning helps finance teams anticipate change, analyze multiple scenarios, and improve cross-functional collaboration. Workday's approach emphasizes business-owned planning processes, quick adaptability, and the use of predictive analytics and agentic AI solutions to drive insights and faster decisions.

feed.title: 3M Stock vs. Honeywell Stock: Which Is A Better Investment?
feed.summary: Despite 3M's strong stock performance this year, driven by strategic improvements and litigation settlements, Honeywell is presented as a better investment. This is due to Honeywell's superior revenue growth, better profitability, and a relatively lower valuation compared to 3M, even though its stock is down due to growth concerns and the complexity of its planned split into three companies. The article suggests a portfolio approach for investors seeking less volatility.

feed.title: Moderna (MRNA) Recently Broke Out Above the 20-Day Moving Average
feed.summary: Moderna (MRNA) has demonstrated a short-term bullish trend after crossing above its 20-day moving average, a key technical indicator for traders. The company also shows a strong positive outlook in earnings estimate revisions, with 9 upward revisions for the current fiscal year. Investors are encouraged to watch MRNA for potential further gains due to this combination of technical and fundamental strength.

feed.title: Nokia outlook changed to positive by Moody’s, ratings affirmed
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has revised Nokia Oyj’s outlook from stable to positive, affirming its Ba1 ratings due to expected improvements in profitability, especially from its network infrastructure segment. The agency anticipates strengthened underlying profitability for Nokia, with robust growth in optical and IP networking driven by demand from hyperscalers and cloud providers, further supported by strategic investments like NVIDIA's equity stake. Despite a muted mobile infrastructure segment, steady recurring profits from intellectual property rights, strong liquidity, and a target for positive free cash flow generation contribute to the positive outlook.

feed.title: Strong E-Commerce Sales a Highlight of Kroger Q3
feed.summary: The Kroger Co. announced strong third-quarter results for 2025, with e-commerce sales surging by 17%. The company reported total sales of $33.9 billion and expects its e-commerce business to become profitable in 2026. Kroger reiterated its commitment to improving customer experience and long-term growth, as well as revising its guidance for identical sales and adjusted EPS.

feed.title: Keysight (NYSE: KEYS) launches FieldFox D-Series for wideband RF signal analysis
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies Inc. (NYSE: KEYS) has launched its new N99xxD-Series FieldFox Handheld Analyzer, enabling 120 MHz gap-free IQ streaming and high-speed data transfer for advanced wideband RF signal analysis. This series combines ruggedness with precision, offering features like AI-powered spectrum management and a touchscreen interface. The FieldFox D-Series aims to provide engineers with enhanced visibility into complex RF environments, accelerating troubleshooting and optimizing system performance.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Secures Contract to Support Aircraft Carrier
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc. (HII) has secured a nearly $91.9 million contract to provide engineering support for the U.S. Navy’s Nimitz-class and Gerald R. Ford-class aircraft carriers, with options potentially raising the value to $472 million. This contract, expected to be completed by November 2030, highlights HII's strong position in the growing naval vessels market. The article also discusses growth prospects for other defense contractors like Lockheed Martin (LMT), BAE Systems plc (BAESY), and General Dynamics (GD) that benefit from increased global defense spending on naval capabilities.

feed.title: JetBlue Airways (JBLU) Receives "Sell" Rating from Citigroup Ana
feed.summary: Citigroup initiated coverage on JetBlue Airways (JBLU) with a "Sell" rating and set a price target of $4.10. This new rating offers a fresh perspective amidst varying analyst opinions on the airline's stock. The average target price from 12 analysts is $4.42, implying a slight downside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $5.70, suggesting an upside.

feed.title: INVESTOR DEADLINE APPROACHING: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of Alexandria Real Estate Equities
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating potential securities claims against Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE: ARE) following allegations that the company made misleading statements about its Long Island City property and reported disappointing third-quarter 2025 financial results. Investors who purchased Alexandria securities between January 27, 2025, and October 27, 2025, are encouraged to contact Faruqi & Faruqi to discuss their legal options before the January 26, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline. The firm highlights a significant stock price drop after Alexandria's Q3 earnings report, which included missed analyst expectations and declines in revenue, adjusted funds from operation, and occupancy rates.

feed.title: Apple, Tesla accused of profiting from abuses, environment destruction
feed.summary: Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc. are facing separate lawsuits alleging "deceptive marketing" by portraying their products as ethically and sustainably manufactured. The lawsuits claim both companies profit from raw materials like cobalt and coltan sourced from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which are linked to child labor, forced labor, and severe human rights and environmental abuses. Both companies are accused of violating consumer protection laws, with Apple denying the claims and Tesla not issuing a response.

feed.title: Equinix inks 10MW virtual PPA in Singapore with TEPCO
feed.summary: Equinix has signed a 20-year, 10MW virtual Power Purchase Agreement (vPPA) with Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) in Singapore. This agreement involves Equinix off-taking power from a portfolio of rooftop solar assets, marking TEPCO's first deal of this kind outside of Japan. The vPPA provides Equinix with energy from renewable sources without direct physical delivery, utilizing a price-hedge mechanism.

feed.title: The Palo Alto Networks Story - ft. Nir Zuk & Nikesh Arora
feed.summary: This article details the evolution of Palo Alto Networks, from Nir Zuk's initial frustration with stagnant cybersecurity to the development of the Next-Generation Firewall. It covers the company's growth, strategic shift under Nikesh Arora with aggressive acquisitions, and its transformation into a comprehensive cybersecurity platform in the cloud era. The narrative highlights key decisions, leadership changes, and the philosophy of disrupting oneself to stay ahead in a rapidly changing industry.

feed.title: Intel makes U-turn, cancels plan to sell its networking division: Read chipmaker’s statement
feed.summary: Intel has reversed its decision to sell or spin off its Networking and Edge Group (NEX) division, opting instead to keep it as an internal unit. This U-turn comes after a financial boost from the US government, SoftBank, and Nvidia, leading the company to believe NEX is best positioned for success within Intel. The chipmaker had initially planned to divest non-core assets to improve its finances amidst competition.

feed.title: Johnson Controls announces quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International plc announced a quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share, payable on January 16, 2026, to shareholders of record by December 22, 2025. This dividend reflects a 1.4% yield and 8.11% growth from the previous year, extending the company's unbroken dividend payment record since 1887. The announcement follows strong fourth-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations in earnings and revenue, with UBS maintaining a 'Buy' rating.

feed.title: The Boeing Company (BA): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on The Boeing Company, highlighting its rebound in commercial aircraft deliveries, improved operational efficiency in its 737 MAX and 787 programs, and expansion in its defense and global services divisions. Key catalysts for future growth include accretive divestitures, the Spirit AeroSystems acquisition, and a substantial $636 billion backlog, positioning Boeing for a significant operational and financial turnaround through 2026–2028.

feed.title: Panagora Asset Management Inc. Acquires 26,667 Shares of Evergy Inc. $EVRG
feed.summary: Panagora Asset Management Inc. significantly increased its stake in Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) by 486.8% during the second quarter, acquiring an additional 26,667 shares and bringing its total holdings to 32,145 shares valued at $2.216 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the energy company, which has seen varied analyst ratings but a consensus "Buy" with an average price target of $80.72. Evergy recently announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.695 per share.

feed.title: Raytheon and AWS collaborate on satellite mission control
feed.summary: Raytheon, an RTX business, has partnered with Amazon Web Services to enhance satellite data processing and mission control operations using cloud technology. This collaboration integrates AWS's AI and machine learning services to reduce mission costs and accelerate capability delivery for government and commercial space systems. The agreement aims to improve mission data processing, edge computing, and constellation command and control while maintaining high security.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on United Airlines Holdings (UAL) with a 'Buy' Rating | UAL Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on United Airlines Holdings (UAL) with a 'Buy' rating, setting a price target of $132.00. This new coverage contributes to a positive trend in recent analyst ratings for UAL, with an average target price of $122.06 from 19 analysts. However, GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $68.62, suggesting a potential downside despite the "Outperform" brokerage recommendation.

feed.title: Copper giant Freeport's CEO won't count on mergers for growth
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan CEO Kathleen Quirk stated that while the company is open to merger and acquisition opportunities, it is not relying on them for growth. Instead, Freeport expects to achieve up to 50% growth in its U.S. business over the next five years through internal organic growth initiatives. Quirk made these remarks at the Reuters NEXT conference in New York.

feed.title: Has FICO’s Long Term Surge Pushed Its Valuation Too Far in 2025?
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) has experienced a significant long-term stock surge but appears overvalued by traditional metrics despite recent pullbacks. The article utilizes Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Earnings ratio analyses, suggesting FICO is currently 83.4% overvalued and trading at a much higher PE multiple than its industry and fair value. It encourages investors to use "Narratives" for a personalized valuation approach.

feed.title: EQT (EQT) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Momentum in the Natural Gas Market
feed.summary: EQT (EQT) has seen strong share price performance recently, with improvements in natural gas market sentiment driving its stock up. The company is trading just below analyst targets and at a discount to some intrinsic value estimates, suggesting it might still be undervalued. However, its valuation on earnings-based metrics appears less favorable compared to industry peers, posing questions about the sustainability of its premium.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO): Reassessing Valuation After Recent Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) has seen its share price rise by 3% in the last month and 18% over three months, prompting a reevaluation of its valuation. Despite trading below analyst targets, the company's strong innovation in analytical tools and AI integration positions it for future revenue and margin growth. While a narrative suggests it is 5.4% undervalued, risks such as persistent weakness in China or funding pressures could impact its performance.

feed.title: Align Showcases Digital Advancements at GNYDM 2025
feed.summary: Align Technology showcased its latest innovations in digital dentistry at the Greater New York Dental Meeting (GNYDM) 2025, highlighting advancements across its Align Digital Platform, including the iTero Lumina scanner, Align Oral Health Suite, and Invisalign Smile Architect. The company and its subsidiary exocad also hosted educational sessions and provided hands-on experiences with their technology. These offerings aim to enhance clinical outcomes, patient treatment experiences, and practice growth for dental professionals.

feed.title: Targa Resources to Acquire Rival Pipeline Stakeholder Midstream for $1.25 Billion
feed.summary: Targa Resources Corp. announced its plan to acquire Stakeholder Midstream for $1.25 billion, a move that will bolster its presence in the Permian Basin and enhance its capacity for oil and natural gas transport and storage. This acquisition aligns with the increasing demand for natural gas to power AI and data centers, as well as the rapid development of LNG terminals. Targa plans to finance the deal using cash and a $3.5 billion line of credit, anticipating approximately $200 million in annual revenue from the acquired assets.

feed.title: November market review
feed.summary: In November, a late surge in the stock market, primarily driven by renewed expectations of a Fed interest rate cut, pushed the S&P 500 and Dow Jones into positive territory for the seventh consecutive month, while the Nasdaq was slightly down. Although there was a mid-month bout of volatility, particularly among AI tech giants, the market outlook for long-term investors remains positive despite elevated valuations. Several key events occurred in November, including the end of a US government shutdown and easing trade tensions between the US and China.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on Southwest Airlines (LUV) with Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on Southwest Airlines (LUV) with a "Neutral" rating and set a price target of $38.00 USD. This marks Citigroup's first rating for the airline, and the market will be observing its impact on investor sentiment. Other analysts have recently maintained or adjusted their ratings and price targets for Southwest Airlines, with the average analyst target suggesting a slight downside, while GuruFocus estimates an upside.

feed.title: Synchrony and Mitsubishi Electric Trane Hvac US Llc Renew Relationship to Offer Customers Flexible Hvac Financing for Greater Comfort
feed.summary: Synchrony and Mitsubishi Electric Trane HVAC US LLC (METUS) have renewed their residential consumer financing partnership, effective October 1, 2025. This multi-year agreement, building on a decade of collaboration, allows METUS customers to access flexible financing options for HVAC systems and services through Synchrony. The partnership aims to enhance the customer purchasing experience and support HVAC contractors by providing tools and new product capabilities, especially as demand for energy-efficient HVAC solutions grows.

feed.title: Micron Technology Soars 178% YTD: Should You Still Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) has seen its shares soar by 177.9% year-to-date, significantly outperforming its peers and the broader tech sector, driven by strong fundamentals and growth in AI, data centers, and automotive sectors. The company's financial performance shows robust revenue and EPS growth, with analysts projecting continued momentum into fiscal 2026. Despite its impressive run, MU stock trades at a lower forward 12-month P/E multiple compared to the sector average and its competitors, suggesting it remains a compelling buy for long-term investors due to its strategic positioning in high-growth tech trends and attractive valuation.

feed.title: How Strong Is AbbVie's Immunology Franchise After Humira's LOE?
feed.summary: AbbVie's immunology franchise has demonstrated robust growth despite Humira's loss of exclusivity (LOE) in 2023, primarily driven by Skyrizi and Rinvoq. These two drugs have exceeded expectations, with combined sales surging 53% year-over-year to $18.5 billion, and are projected to reach over $31 billion by 2027 due to market share gains and new indications. AbbVie is also expanding its presence in oncology and neuroscience, while facing competition from other key players like Johnson & Johnson and Eli Lilly in the immunology market.
